rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILTON WISE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. UN-endorsed filed copies of new "declarations" by the attorney, not the Court-appointed fiduciary [CRC 7.103(c)], were dropped off to the Examiner in response to the preliminary tentative ruling. The Court to determine if the estate can close and be finally distributed, as requested. The first appointed executor has not declined her share of statutory compensation. [CRC 7.704.] Notice was mailed to the trustee of the Williams Family Trust, purported successor-in-interest to Yvonne Williams, but no 13100 affidavit with an acceptance of trusteeship was filed by him. [LRSF 14.67 I.] As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. 13100 AFFIDAVITS MUST BE FILED CONVENTIONALLY TO CONFIRM THAT AN ORIGINAL CERTIFIED COPY OF THE DEATH CERTIFICATE IS ATTACHED. [LRSF 14.93 E.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF FELIX ZAMUDIO JUAREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Main issue is the request for fees. It is unclear if time entries were duplicated between this case and the trust, and some of the entries appear quite high for this small estate with few transactions (disbursements). Time spent to amend the first accounting petition merely to request the fees that were not set forth in the petition, as filed, should not be the burden of the estate unless there was a valid reason for filing an incomplete petition without the fees disclosed. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEBORAH WHALLEY-KENDRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. The Court to determine if the petition should be granted as filed and supplemented, although not all of the issues noted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 11/3/20 have been addressed yet and there are additional issues following the 11/16/20 filing of the Final I&A and supplement. Some of the issues to be addressed: (1) the Final I&A filed on 11/16/20 appears to have an incorrect total; (2) the total of the four I&As does not agree with either the amount shown on the Summary of Account or in the fee base calculation; (3) neither the petition nor the supplement filed on 11/16/20 shows the description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to the heir, as required by CRC 7.651 and LRSF 14.67 L.7. (4) it appears that the administrator invested, rather than preserved, assets of the estate; (5) it appears that the attorney was paid fees without a Court order; (6) the full statement required by Probate Code Section 9202(b) still has not been made; and (7) the last page of a proposed order cannot have just the judgeâs signature [LRSF 14.20 C.] CONTRARY TO COUNSEL'S STATEMENT AT THE HEARING: NO 65-PAGE PLEADING WAS FILED BEFORE THIS HEARING.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HERBERT VILLAFLORES ARCEO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/19/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) No statement verifying the ending balance of the Chevron retirement account value has been filed. 2) Receipts signed by the successor conservators for the transferred assets described on Schedule C-1 to the supplemental account have not been filed, except for the Wells Fargo CD & Bank of America checking accounts. 3) The petitioner's fee declaration does not address all factors under CRC 7.756(a). In particular, the petitioner's time sheets, though voluminous, are not self-explanatory for the necessity factor under CRC 7.756(a)(3). There is at least one entry on almost each business day. 4) Re Exhibit H: i) Please explain the purpose of the $168.90 cost incurred on 07/30/2019. ii) Should the services to correct the name on the appointment order be compensated from the estate?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAWN LYNETTE NELSON-NOYES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/21. No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.6 A., and 14.93 G.] A quick skim of the petition online turned up several issues: there were no disclaimers attached to the petition or filed separately; if the other two heirs disclaimed, their interest passes to their issue, not to their brother, but the petition does not address whether or not Savanna Robson or Cassidy Noyes have living issue; Cassidy Noyes and Savanna Robson are apparently not residents of the US, but if they are citizens of a foreign country, notice was never given to the consular official of that foreign country maintaining an office in the U.S., as required by Probate Code Section 8113; attorney costs were not sufficiently itemized (no dates, missing payees or inadequate descriptions, etc.). The petition has not been reviewed in detail. Courtesy copies of the petition and any subsequent papers filed to address these or other issues and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/15/21 to avoid an automatic continuance. (Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be checked or answered with the date of the order appointing the Probate Referee when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.)
